Lines.FullText: An input port can filter out incompatible signals by returning false when such a signal is passed as argument

Elements.RawText: An input port can filter out incompatible signals by returning false when such a signal is passed as argument

Lines.FullText: to `acceptsSignal`, and also rejects the signals when they are passed as argument to `connect`.

Elements.RawText: to `acceptsSignal`, and also rejects the signals when they are passed as argument to `connect`.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * An input port can filter out incompatible signals by returning false when such a signal is passed as argument
 * to `acceptsSignal`, and also rejects the signals when they are passed as argument to `connect`.

Lines.FullText: Depending on the configuration, an input port might not require a signal to be connected, returning false

Elements.RawText: Depending on the configuration, an input port might not require a signal to be connected, returning false

Lines.FullText: when `requiresSignal` is called. Such input ports are usually a part of function blocks that do not require

Elements.RawText: when `requiresSignal` is called. Such input ports are usually a part of function blocks that do not require

Lines.FullText: a given signal to perform calculations.

Elements.RawText: a given signal to perform calculations.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * Depending on the configuration, an input port might not require a signal to be connected, returning false
 * when `requiresSignal` is called. Such input ports are usually a part of function blocks that do not require
 * a given signal to perform calculations.

Lines.FullText: Any openDAQ object which wishes to receive signal data must create an input port and connect it to said

Elements.RawText: Any openDAQ object which wishes to receive signal data must create an input port and connect it to said

Lines.FullText: signals. Such objects are for example function blocks, and readers.

Elements.RawText: signals. Such objects are for example function blocks, and readers.

Description.RawText:
*
 * Any openDAQ object which wishes to receive signal data must create an input port and connect it to said
 * signals. Such objects are for example function blocks, and readers.
